P88.itemDescription: person or organization that commissioned this work

P123.itemDescription: organization or person responsible for publishing a work, such as a book, periodical, printed music, podcast, game or software

P1365.itemDescription: person, state or item replaced. Use "structure replaces" (P1398) for structures. Use "follows" (P155) if the previous item was not replaced or predecessor and successor are identical

P186.itemDescription: material the subject or the object is made of or derived from (do not confuse with P10672 which is used for processes)

P6275.itemDescription: person or organisation who represents the copyright for this person or work of art

P641.itemDescription: sport that the subject participates or participated in or is associated with

P279.itemDescription: this item is a subclass (subset) of that item; ALL instances of this item are instances of that item; different from P31 (instance of), e.g.: volcano is a subclass of mountain; Everest is an instance of mountain

P1366.itemDescription: other person or item which continues the item by replacing it in its role. Use P156 ("followed by") if the item is not replaced nor identical, but adds to the series (e.g. books in a series).

P559.itemDescription: the feature (intersecting road, train station, etc.) at the end of a linear feature

P156.itemDescription: immediately following item in a series of which the subject is a part, preferably use as qualifier of P179 [if the subject has been replaced, e.g. political offices, use "replaced by" (P1366)]

P2341.itemDescription: place or ethnic group where a language, art genre, cultural tradition or expression, cooking style or food, or biological species or variety is found (or was originally found)

P485.itemDescription: the institution holding the subject's archives

P19.itemDescription: most specific known birth location of a person, animal or fictional character

P749.itemDescription: parent organization or unit of an organization or unit, opposite of child organization or unit (P355); use instance of (P31) to distinguish organization (Q43229) and organization unit (Q10387680)

P112.itemDescription: founder or co-founder of this organization, religion, place or entity

P800.itemDescription: notable scientific, artistic or literary work, or other work of significance among subject's works

P1552.itemDescription: inherent or distinguishing quality or feature of the entity. Use a more specific property when possible

P155.itemDescription: immediately prior item in a series of which the subject is a part, preferably use as qualifier of P179 [if the subject has replaced the preceding item, e.g. political offices, use "replaces" (P1365)]

P750.itemDescription: distributor of a creative work; distributor for a record label; news agency; film distributor

P1343.itemDescription: work where this item is described

P277.itemDescription: the programming language(s) in which the software is developed

P306.itemDescription: operating system (OS) on which a software works or the OS installed on hardware

P6291.itemDescription: cause, organization or product targeted by the advertising campaign, character, jingle or slogan

P1066.itemDescription: person who has taught this person

P5009.itemDescription: the product or work complies with a certain norm or passes a test

P166.itemDescription: award or recognition received by a person, organization or creative work

P7888.itemDescription: the subject was dissolved and merged into the object, that existed previously

P361.itemDescription: object of which the subject is a part (if this subject is already part of object A which is a part of object B, then please only make the subject part of object A), inverse property of "has part" (P527, see also "has parts of the class" (P2670))

P50.itemDescription: main creator(s) of a written work (use on works, not humans); use P2093 (author name string) when Wikidata item is unknown or does not exist

P162.itemDescription: person(s) who produced the film, musical work, theatrical production, etc. (for film, this does not include executive producers, associate producers, etc.) [for production company, use P272, video games - use P178]

P1071.itemDescription: place where the item was conceived or made; where applicable, location of final assembly

P108.itemDescription: person or organization for which the subject works or worked

P178.itemDescription: organization or person that developed the item

P451.itemDescription: someone with whom the person is in a relationship without being married. Use "spouse" (P26) for married couples

P527.itemDescription: part of this subject; inverse property of "part of" (P361). See also "has parts of the class" (P2670).

P40.itemDescription: subject has object as child. Do not use for stepchildren—use "relative" (P1038), qualified with "type of kinship" (P1039)

P706.itemDescription: located on the specified (geo)physical feature. Should not be used when the value is only political/administrative (P131) or a mountain range (P4552). Use P206 for things in/on bodies of water.

P1398.itemDescription: the item this building or structure replaced, at the same geographic location

P1889.itemDescription: item that is different from another item, with which it may be confused

P1444.itemDescription: destination for this route (journey, flight, sailing, exploration, migration, etc.)

P2813.itemDescription: media that speaks for an organization or a movement, and that is usually edited by its members

P144.itemDescription: the work(s) or inputs used as the basis for subject item; for fictional analog use P1074

P138.itemDescription: entity or event that inspired the subject's name, or namesake (in at least one language). Qualifier "applies to name" (P5168) can be used to indicate which one

P2770.itemDescription: source of income of an organization or person

P802.itemDescription: notable student(s) of the subject individual

P1427.itemDescription: starting place of this journey, flight, voyage, trek, migration etc.

P101.itemDescription: specialization of a person or organization; see P106 for the occupation

P3173.itemDescription: things, places this place offers views on

P1056.itemDescription: material or product, including services, produced or provided by an organization, industry, facility, or process

P1478.itemDescription: nearest, proximate thing that directly resulted in the subject as outcome or effect. Used in conjunction with 'has cause' (i.e. underlying cause) and 'has contributing factor'. See 'Help:Modeling causes'.

P5607.itemLabel: located in the religious territorial entity
P5607.itemDescription: the item is located on the territory of the following religious entity. Use P708 (diocese) for dioceses

P3373.itemDescription: the subject and the object have at least one common parent (brother, sister, etc. including half-siblings); use "relative" (P1038) for siblings-in-law (brother-in-law, sister-in-law, etc.) and step-siblings (step-brothers, step-sisters, etc.)

P740.itemDescription: location where a group or organization was formed

P2348.itemDescription: time period (historic period or era, sports season, theatre season, legislative period etc.) in which the subject occurred or with which it is associated

P1269.itemDescription: topic of which this item is an aspect; item that offers a broader perspective on the same topic

P1038.itemDescription: family member (qualify with "kinship to subject", P1039; for direct family member please use specific property)

P2789.itemDescription: item with which the item is physically connected

P452.itemDescription: specific industry of company or organization

P1535.itemDescription: item or concept that makes use of the subject (use sub-properties when appropriate)

P3018.itemDescription: protected area where a place or geographical feature is physically located

P31.itemDescription: type to which this subject corresponds/belongs. Different from P279 (subclass of); for example: K2 is an instance of mountain; volcano is a subclass of mountain

P5940.itemDescription: a narrative of this creative work is expanded in another work while '''not necessarily''' being part of the same series. For series use P179 instead

P5869.itemDescription: defines which item is a best practice example of modelling a subject, which is described by the value of this property, usage instructions at Wikidata:Model items

P941.itemDescription: work, human, place or event which inspired this creative work or fictional entity

P2578.itemDescription: the object that an academic field studies; distinct from field of work (P101), which is used for human, organization, etc.

P8956.itemDescription: this work, product, object or standard can interact with another work, product, object or standard

P553.itemDescription: website that the person or organization has an account on (use with P554) Note: only used with reliable source or if the person or organization disclosed it.

P197.itemDescription: the stations next to this station, sharing the same line(s)

P81.itemDescription: railway line(s) subject is directly connected to

P463.itemDescription: organization, club or musical group to which the subject belongs. Do not use for membership in ethnic or social groups, nor for holding a political position, such as a member of parliament (use P39 for that)

P190.itemDescription: twin towns, sister cities, twinned municipalities and other localities that have a partnership or cooperative agreement, either legally or informally acknowledged by their governments

P1068.itemDescription: set of machine code instructions on which a processor architecture is based

P1441.itemDescription: this (fictional or fictionalized) entity, place, or person appears in that work as part of the narration (use P2860 for works citing other works, P361/P1433 for works being part of other works, P1343 for entities described in non-fictional accounts)

P807.itemDescription: subject was founded or started by separating from identified object

P126.itemDescription: person or organization in charge of keeping the subject (for instance an infrastructure) in functioning order

P1582.itemDescription: links a natural product with its source (animal, plant, fungal, algal, etc.)

P2652.itemDescription: partnership (commercial or/and non-commercial) between this organization and another organization or institution

P710.itemDescription: person, group of people or organization (object) that actively takes/took part in an event or process (subject). Preferably qualify with "object has role" (P3831). Use P1923 for participants that are teams.

P1376.itemDescription: country, state, department, canton or other administrative division of which the municipality is the governmental seat

P2546.itemDescription: close companion of a fictional character

P8411.itemDescription: environment (geographic feature, building type, etc.) the narrative work is set in

P1672.itemDescription: links a taxon to natural products it produces. Note that it does not say "this taxon is the source of" or "this taxon is a source of" as this may vary. Some products may be yielded by more than one taxon.

P195.itemDescription: art, museum, archival, or bibliographic collection of which the subject is part (item is in the collection of X)

P3931.itemDescription: person or organisation who holds the copyright of a work according to the Berne Convention

P4969.itemDescription: new work of art (film, book, software, etc.) derived from major part of this work

P355.itemDescription: child organization/unit of a organization/unit; for companies, generally a fully owned separate corp., see business division (P199); opposite of parent org./unit (P749); use instance of (P31) to distinguish org. (Q43229) and org. unit (Q10387680)

P287.itemDescription: person or organization which designed the object. For buildings use "architect" (Property:P84)

P629.itemDescription: is an edition or translation of this entity

P859.itemDescription: organization or individual that sponsors this item

P1001.itemDescription: the item (institution, law, public office, public register, etc) or statement belongs to or has power over or applies to the value (a territorial jurisdiction: a country, state, municipality, etc)

P8001.itemDescription: public support given by a person or organization to a political or other campaign

P664.itemDescription: person or institution organizing an event

P8363.itemDescription: classification system for clinical trial, experiment, research

P1299.itemDescription: object depicting this subject (object being creative works such as books, films, paintings)

P414.itemDescription: exchange on which this company is traded

P937.itemDescription: location where persons or organisations were actively participating in employment, business or other work

P17.itemDescription: sovereign state that this item is in (not to be used for human beings)

P2670.itemDescription: the subject has one or more parts of the object class

P2210.itemDescription: qualifier: what a statement value is relative to

P159.itemDescription: city or town where an organization's headquarters is or has been situated. Use P276 qualifier for specific building

P1434.itemDescription: the subject is a work describing a fictional universe, i.e. whose plot occurs in this universe

P9234.itemDescription: subject work or edition updates or otherwise complements the object work or edition

P10754.itemDescription: place or location where a good or service is distributed from

P180.itemDescription: entity visually depicted in an image, literarily described in a work, or otherwise incorporated into an audiovisual or other medium; see also P921, 'main subject'

P1557.itemDescription: inherent and characteristic embodiment of a given concept

P1716.itemDescription: commercial brand associated with the item

P8431.itemDescription: course type of a food dish (appetizer, dessert, drink, main course, salad, side dish)

P2283.itemDescription: item or concept used by the subject or in the operation (see also instrument [P1303] and armament [P520])

P167.itemDescription: the item which replaced this building or structure, at the same geographic location

P2512.itemDescription: works that are spin-offs of this item

P2159.itemDescription: problem that this algorithm or method solves

P2541.itemDescription: geographic area or jurisdiction an organisation or industry operates in, serves, or has responsibility for

P797.itemDescription: entity having executive power on given entity

P921.itemDescription: primary topic of a work or act of communication

P501.itemDescription: territory is entirely surrounded (enclaved) by the other territory

P4584.itemDescription: work in which a fictional/mythical character or entity first appeared

P437.itemDescription: method (or type) of distribution for the subject

P2094.itemDescription: official classification by a regulating body under which the subject (events, teams, participants, or equipment) qualifies for inclusion

P301.itemDescription: primary topic of the subject Wikimedia category

P135.itemDescription: literary, artistic, scientific or philosophical movement or scene associated with this person or work. For political ideologies use P1142.

P366.itemDescription: main use of the subject (includes current and former usage)

P241.itemDescription: branch to which this military unit, award, office, or person belongs, e.g. Royal Navy

P25.itemDescription: female parent of the subject. For stepmother, use "stepparent" (P3448)

P8571.itemDescription: external company or person appointed to audit a business' or an organization's books, requirement for many companies

P36.itemDescription: seat of government of a country, province, state or other type of administrative territorial entity

P461.itemDescription: item that is in some way the opposite of this item

P7719.itemDescription: theory/notion/statement/algorithm which in some sense this theory/notion/statement/algorithm generalizes

P2416.itemDescription: discipline an athlete competed in within a sport

P479.itemDescription: input device used to interact with a software or a device

P136.itemDescription: creative work's genre or an artist's field of work (P101). Use main subject (P921) to relate creative works to their topic

P2959.itemDescription: this item duplicates another item and the two can't be merged, as one Wikimedia project includes two pages, e.g. in different scripts or languages (only applies to some wikis, e.g.: cdowiki, gomwiki, nanwiki). Use P31=Q17362920 for other wikis.

P2868.itemDescription: role held by the item the statement appears on ("subject") in the context of that statement. For the role of the statement object/value, use P3831 ("object has role"). For acting roles, use P453 ("character role"). For persons, use P39.

P495.itemDescription: country of origin of this item (creative work, food, phrase, product, etc.)

P460.itemLabel: said to be the same as
P460.itemDescription: this item is said to be the same as that item, though this may be uncertain or disputed

P7047.itemDescription: opponent character or group of this fictive character or group

P199.itemDescription: organizational divisions of this organization (which are not independent legal entities)

P47.itemDescription: countries or administrative subdivisions, of equal level, that this item borders, either by land or water. A single common point is enough.

P3095.itemDescription: type of agents that study this subject or work in this field

P751.itemDescription: feature introduced by this version of a product item

P400.itemDescription: platform for which a work was developed or released, or the specific platform version of a software product

P2992.itemDescription: quality assurance process in place for a particular software

P137.itemDescription: person, profession, organization or entity that operates the equipment, facility, or service

P828.itemDescription: underlying cause, entity that ultimately resulted in this effect

P2650.itemDescription: item of special or vested interest to this person or organisation

P1080.itemDescription: subject's fictional entity is in the object narrative. See also P1441 (present in work) and P1445 (fictional universe described in)

P1414.itemDescription: framework or toolkit a program uses to display the graphical user interface

P8324.itemDescription: entity that gives money to a person, organization, or project for a specific purpose

P175.itemDescription: actor, musician, band or other performer associated with this role or musical work

P767.itemLabel: contributor to the creative work or subject
P767.itemDescription: person or organization that contributed to a subject: co-creator of a creative work or subject

P880.itemDescription: central processing unit found within the subject item

P610.itemDescription: point with highest elevation in a region or path

P1416.itemDescription: organization that a person or organization is affiliated with (not necessarily member of or employed by)

P106.itemDescription: occupation of a person. See also "field of work" (Property:P101), "position held" (Property:P39). Not for groups of people. There, use "field of work" (Property:P101), "industry" (Property:P452), "members have occupation" (Property:P3989).

P6440.itemDescription: method or steps that maintain, clean up, or repair this item and lengthens its lifespan

P1383.itemDescription: settlement which an administrative division contains

P4387.itemDescription: method used by an app/OS to receive updates or self-update

P3447.itemDescription: the website automatically crawls or mirrors another website as its data source

P2851.itemDescription: types of payment accepted by a venue

P176.itemDescription: (main or final) manufacturer or producer of this product

P912.itemDescription: the subject item has this type of facility, e.g. toilet, car park

P4552.itemDescription: range or subrange to which the geographical item belongs

P150.itemDescription: (list of) direct subdivisions of an administrative territorial entity

P2184.itemDescription: item about the historical development of a subject's topic, example: "history of Argentina" for "Argentina". To list key events of the topic, use "significant event" (P793)

P13047.itemDescription: person or organisation that holds or was granted a lease on the subject

P248.itemDescription: to be used in the references field to refer to the information document or database in which a claim is made; for qualifiers use P805; for the type of document in which a claim is made use P3865

P276.itemDescription: location of the object, structure or event; use P131 to indicate the containing administrative entity, P8138 for statistical entities, or P706 for geographic entities; use P7153 for locations associated with the object

P3158.itemDescription: the type of structure enclosing another structure

P1408.itemDescription: place that a radio/TV station is licensed/required to broadcast to

P425.itemDescription: field corresponding to this occupation or profession (use only for occupations/professions - for people use Property:P101, for companies use P452)

P408.itemDescription: software engine employed by the subject item

P57.itemDescription: director(s) of film, TV-series, stageplay, video game or similar

P1382.itemDescription: object that partially overlaps with the subject in its instances, parts, or members

P7959.itemDescription: traditional, geographical division of Great Britain and Ireland

P275.itemDescription: license under which this copyrighted work is released

P2500.itemDescription: the league below this sports league

P2633.itemDescription: item that deals with the geography of the subject. Sample: "Rio de Janeiro" uses this property with value "geography of Rio de Janeiro" (Q10288853). For the location of a subject, use "location" (P276).

P1142.itemDescription: political ideology of an organization or person or of a work (such as a newspaper)

P10568.itemDescription: this collection resource maintains a consistent linking to this other collection resource

P747.itemDescription: item that is an edition or translation of this item

P3342.itemDescription: person linked to the item in any possible way

P1445.itemDescription: to link a fictional universe with a work that describes it: <universe> "described in the work:" <work>

P2499.itemDescription: the league above this sports league

P4330.itemDescription: contents, item or substance located within this wrapping item, but not part of this receptacle or container; does not describe the ingredients or components of this container

P674.itemDescription: characters which appear in this item (like plays, operas, operettas, books, comics, films, TV series, video games)

P2825.itemDescription: intermediate point on a journey - stopover location, waystation or routing point

P8345.itemDescription: this creative work belongs to this media franchise

P1433.itemDescription: larger work that a given work was published in, like a journal, a website, a collection, a book or a music album

P551.itemDescription: the place where the person is or has been, resident

P3719.itemDescription: organization that acts as regulator of an activity, financial market, or stock exchange

P737.itemDescription: the subject (person, idea, etc.) was influenced or inspired by this object entity, e.g. "Heidegger was influenced by Aristotle"

P4290.itemDescription: official app for this organization or other entity (a downloadable application for Android, iOS, etc.)

P1547.itemDescription: subject software depends on object software

P2596.itemDescription: human culture or people (or several cultures) associated with this item

P4428.itemDescription: implementation of a standard, program, specification or programming language

P10695.itemDescription: work that introduced this concept or feature

P449.itemDescription: network(s) or service(s) that originally broadcast a radio or television program

P840.itemDescription: the narrative of the work is set in this location

P2079.itemDescription: method, process or technique used to grow, cook, weave, build, assemble, manufacture the item

P22.itemDescription: male parent of the subject. For stepfather, use "stepparent" (P3448)

P131.itemLabel: located in the administrative territorial entity
P131.itemDescription: the item is located on the territory of the following administrative entity. Use P276 for specifying locations that are non-administrative places and for items about events. Use P1382 if the item falls only partially into the administrative entity.

P272.itemDescription: company that produced this film, audio or performing arts work

P618.itemDescription: describes the source of energy an animated object (machine or animal) uses

P3113.itemDescription: expected part that the item does not have (for qualities, use P6477)

P2361.itemDescription: online service or online service provider tied to an item

P11197.itemDescription: build system used by a software during its development

P5008.itemLabel: on focus list of Wikimedia project
P5008.itemDescription: property to indicate that an item is of particular interest for a Wikimedia project. This property does not add notability. Items should not be created with this property if they are not notable for Wikidata. See also P6104, P972, P2354.

P121.itemDescription: equipment, installation or service operated by the subject

P26.itemDescription: the subject has the object as their spouse (husband, wife, partner, etc.). Use "unmarried partner" (P451) for non-married companions

P291.itemDescription: geographical place of publication of the edition (use 1st edition when referring to works)

P793.itemDescription: significant or notable events associated with the subject

P2860.itemDescription: citation from one creative or scholarly work to another

P915.itemDescription: actual place where this scene/film was shot. For the setting, use "narrative location" (P840)

P170.itemDescription: maker of this creative work or other object (where no more specific property exists)

P2579.itemDescription: subject is studied by this science or domain

P6477.itemDescription: expected quality that the item does not possess
